description_long: This object is a vintage-style gramophone record player with a large, flared brass horn mounted on a hexagonal wooden base. The base features ornate carved details and four small columns supporting an open compartment beneath the turntable area. The turntable platter is circular, dark, and textured, occupying the center of the top surface. The tonearm, made of metal, curves elegantly from the base toward the platter, with a visible joint where it meets the horn. The wood has a rich, dark brown finish, while the horn is a muted brass with a slightly weathered patina. No visible control knobs or dust cover are present, giving the object a classic, mechanical appearance.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large brass horn, ornate wooden hexagonal base, and a dark, textured turntable platter.
description_view_0: The record player is seen from above, highlighting the hexagonal wooden base, the circular platter, and the sweeping brass horn extending to the left.
description_view_1: The front view shows the wide opening of the brass horn, the tonearm curving over the platter, and the detailed wooden base with visible columns.
description_view_2: The side view emphasizes the length of the horn and the open compartment beneath the platter, with the tonearm visible along the side.
description_view_3: A top-down angle into the horn, showing its flared interior and the platter partially obscured by the horn's rim.
attribute_turntable_platter: Present and centrally located, the turntable platter is circular, dark in color, and appears to be made of metal or a dense composite. It has a textured, grooved surface, typical of vintage record players, and occupies about one-third of the top surface area. No distinct markings or labels are visible.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, made of metal, and curves from the base toward the platter. It is medium in length, with a visible joint at the base and a dark finish. The tonearm is positioned over the platter, suggesting it is ready for use.
